title: quercus lusitanica subsp. baetica
intro: Quercus lusitanica subsp. baetica, also known as the Iberian oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a type of evergreen tree that belongs to the Quercus genus, which is known for its medicinal properties. The leaves and bark of Quercus lusitanica subsp. baetica contain tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Quercus lusitanica subsp. baetica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections.
